Event (Latin): Bellum Trōiānum inceptum est.
Event (English): The Trojan War is started.

Event (Latin): Bellum Trōiānum perfectum est.
Event (English): The Trojan War is ended.

Event (Latin): Urbs Roma condita est.
Event (English): The city of Rome is founded.

Event (Latin): Feminae Sabinae a viris Romanis raptae sunt.
Event (English): The Rape of the Sabine Women by the men of Rome.

Event (Latin): Ultimus Rex Sabinōrum, Titus Tatius, mortuus est
Event (English): The Last king of the Sabines, Titus Tatius dies

Event (Latin): Romulus deus factus est
Event (English): Romulus is deified

Event (Latin): Numa Pompilius rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Numa Pompilius is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Tullus Hostilius rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Tullus Hostilius is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Ancus Marcius rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Ancus Marcius is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Lucius Tarquinius Priscus rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Lucius Tarquinius Priscus is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Cloaca Maxima aedificata est
Event (English): The Cloaca Maxima is built

Event (Latin): Servius Tullius rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Servius Tullius is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Lucius Tarquinius Superbus rex Romanus factus est
Event (English): Lucius Tarquinius Superbus is made king of Rome

Event (Latin): Templum Iovī Optimō Maximō in monte Capitolīnō aedificatum est
Event (English): The Temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus is built on the Capitoline Hill

Event (Latin): Res publica Romana constituta est
Event (English): The Roman Republic is established

Event (Latin): Tarquinius Superbus, ultimus rex Romae, necātus est
Event (English): Tarquin the Proud, the last king of Rome, is killed

Event (Latin): Aedes Mercuriī in Monte Aventinō prope Circo Maximo aedificatum est
Event (English): The Temple of Mercury is built on the Aventine Hill near to the Circus Maximus

Event (Latin): Virgō Vestalis, nomine Oppia, incestum accusata, occisa est
Event (English): A Vestal Virgin, named Oppia, accused of inchastity, is put to death

Event (Latin): Cincinnatus dictator factus est ut contrā Aequōs pugnāret.
Event (English): Cincinnati is made dictator in order to fight the Aequi.

Event (Latin): Leges, nomine Duodecim Tabulae, scriptae et in rostrīs positae sunt
Event (English): The Laws called twelve tables are written and publice displayed on the rostra

Event (Latin): Matrimonium inter patricios plebeiosque nondum vetatum est
Event (English): Marriage between Patricians and Plebians is made legal

Event (Latin): Milites Romanī primum salarium accipiunt
Event (English): Roman Soldiers first receive a salary

Event (Latin): Templum Junoni Monetae in monte Capitolīnō sacrātum est
Event (English): The Temple of Juno Moneta is dedicated

Event (Latin): Primus Consul plebeius, Lucius Sextius Lateranus, electus est
Event (English): The first plebeian consul, Lucius Sextius Lateranus, is elected

Event (Latin): Alexander Magnus, rex Macedoniae, Babyloniae mortuus est
Event (English): Alexander the Great, king of Macendonia, died in Babylon

Event (Latin): Ap. Claudius Caecus primam viam, Viam Appiam, aquaeductumque, Aquam Appiam, aedificavit
Event (English): Appius Claudius Caecus builds the first road, the Appian Way, and the first aqueduct, the Aqua Appia

Event (Latin): Plebeibus sacerdotes fieri nondum vetati sunt
Event (English): Plebeians are allowed to become priests

Event (Latin): Bellum contrā Pyrrhum, regem Epirī, perfectum est
Event (English): The War against Pyrrhus, king of Epirus, ends

Event (Latin): Primum Bellum Punicum contrā Carthaginem inceptum est
Event (English): The First Punic War against Carthage begins

Event (Latin): Roma Siciliam vincit et provinciam facit
Event (English): Rome conquers Sicily and makes it a province

Event (Latin): Secundum Bellum Punicum contrā Carthaginem inceptum est
Event (English): The Second Punic War against Carthage begins

Event (Latin): Q. Fabius Maximus dictator exercitum Romanum contra Carthaginem duxit ratione strategicā fatigationis et proelia negante dictā 'Fabiānā' utēns
Event (English): Quintus Fabius Maximus Verrucosus, a dictator, led the Roman army against the Carthaginians using the so-called Fabian Strategy of exhaustion and denying battle

Event (Latin): Hannibal Exercitum Romanum in proeliō Cannae caecidit
Event (English): Hannibal defeats Rome at the battle of Cannae

Event (Latin): Templum Concordiae consecratum est
Event (English): The Temple of Concord is consecrated

Event (Latin): Romānī a Oraculō Sibyllinō Magnam Matrem in pantheum deōrum Romanum adoptare monītī sunt ut Carthaginem destruerent
Event (English): Rome is told to adopt the Great Mother into the Roman Pantheon by the Sibylline Oracle, so that Rome destroy Carthage

Event (Latin): Scipio Africanus Hannibalem et Carthaginensēs apud Zamam vicit
Event (English): Scipio Africanus defeats Hannibal and Carthaginians at Zama

Event (Latin): Roma Hispaniam a Carthagine cepit et in formam provinciae redegit
Event (English): Rome takes Hispania from Carthage

Event (Latin): Ennius poeta, qui poema Annales de origine Roma usque ad vitam Catonis scripserat, mortuus est
Event (English): The poet Ennius, who had written the poem Annales about the origins of Rome up to the life of Cato, died

Event (Latin): P. Terentius Afer, poeta et libertus qui multas comoedias scripserat, vicesimo sexto aetatis suae annō mortuus est
Event (English): Terence, a poet and a freedman who wrote comedies, died at the age of 25

Event (Latin): Cato Maior, qui insigniter "Carthaginem delendam esse" dixerat, mortuus est
Event (English): Cato the Elder, famous for saying "Carthage must be Destroyed", died

Event (Latin): Claudia virgo Vestalis patrem suum, Claudium Pulchrum, qui triumphum celebrabat, a plebeis eum oppugnantibus defendit
Event (English): The Vestal Virgin Claudia defends her father Claudius Pulcher from the plebs who attacked him while he was celebrating his triumph

Event (Latin): Gaius Tiberius Gracchus ab Optimatibus necātus est propter leges populares quas ipse Tribunus plebis anno praeterito promovit
Event (English): Gaius Tiberius Gracchus, Tribune of the Plebs, is killed by the Optimates because of laws he had passed the previous year as Tribune of the Plebs.

Event (Latin): L. Coelius Antipater, qui historiam de secundō bellō Punicō scripserat, mortuus est
Event (English): Lucius Coelius Antipater, who had written a history of the Second Punic War, died

Event (Latin): Tres Virgines Vestales, nomine Aemilia, Marcia, Liciniaque, propter incestum occisae sunt
Event (English): Three Vestal Virgins, called Aemilia, Marcia, and Licinia, are killed for violating their oath of chastity

Event (Latin): Gaius Marius legiones Romanas reformavit. Militēs, quī postquam arma ferre ipsī debebant, "mulī Mariānī" dictī sunt.
Event (English): Gaius Marius reformed the Roman Military. His soldiers, who afterward had to carry their own weapons and equipment, were called "Marius' mules."

Event (Latin): Bellum sociale, inter sociōs Romae Italōs et Rem publicam Romānam, inceptum est
Event (English): The Social War, between Rome and its Italian allies, begins

Event (Latin): Sulla armātus pomerium transiit
Event (English): Sulla crosses the Pomerium under arms

Event (Latin): Gaius Marius mortuus est
Event (English): Gaius Marius died

Event (Latin): Sulla sē dictatorem facit
Event (English): Sulla makes himself dictator

Event (Latin): Spartacus servos liberat et in bello contrā Rōmam ducit
Event (English): Spartacus frees slaves and leads them in rebellion against Rome

Event (Latin): Fabia, Vestalis Maxima, ā Cicerōne criminis incestī cum Catalinā accusata est sed innocēns iudicāta et nōn punita est
Event (English): Fabia, the Chief Vestal Virgin, is prosecuted by Cicero for violating her vows of chastity with Cataline but is proclaimed innocent and not punished

Event (Latin): Piratae Ciliciani a Pompeiō Magnō victi sunt
Event (English): The Cilician Pirates are defeated by Pompey the Great

Event (Latin): Iulius Caesar pontifex maximus electus est
Event (English): Julius Caesar is elected Pontifex Maximus

Event (Latin): Cicero coniurationem Catalinae inhibit
Event (English): Cicero stops the Cataline Conspiracy

Event (Latin): C. Sallustius Crispus librum Bellum Catalinae scribit
Event (English): Sallust writes the book The Cataline War

Event (Latin): Pompeius Magnus Ierusaleum vicit
Event (English): Pompey defeats the rebellion in Jerusalem

Event (Latin): Primus triumviratus inter Crassum, Pompeium Magnum, et Iulium Caesarem factus est
Event (English): The first triumvirate is formed among Crassus, Pompey the Great, and Julius Caesar

Event (Latin): Iulius Caesar cum Marcō Bibulō consul est
Event (English): Julius Caesar is consul with Marcus Bibulus

Event (Latin): Caesar Helvetiōs oppugnat et Bellum Gallicum incipit.
Event (English): Caesar attacks the Helvetii begining the Gallic War.

Event (Latin): Terentia multās epistulās marito suō Ciceronī qui in exilio erat scribit
Event (English): Terentia writes many letters to her husband Cicero who was in exile

Event (Latin): Iulius Caesar ad Britanniam it. Est prīmus Rōmānus quī ad illam īnsulam advenit.
Event (English): Julius Caesar goes to Britain. He is the first Roman to go to the island.

Event (Latin): Catullus poeta, qui centum et sedecim poema scripserat, mortuus esse dicitur
Event (English): The poet Catullua, who wrote 116 poems, is said to have died

Event (Latin): Crassus a Parthis occisus est apud Carrhas
Event (English): Crassus is killed by the Parthians at Carrhae

Event (Latin): Vercingetorix a Caesare victus et captus est in proelio apud Alesiam
Event (English): Vercingetorix is defeated and captured by Caesar at the battle of Alesia

Event (Latin): "Alea iacta est." Caesar flūmen Rubicōnem transit et bellum civīle contrā Pompeium et Senātum Rōmānum incipit.
Event (English): "The die is cast." Caesar crosses the river Rubicon and begins the civil war against Pompey and the Roman Senate.

Event (Latin): Caesar Rōmae dictator sē facit.
Event (English): Caesar makes himself dictator in Rome

Event (Latin): Proelium apud Pharsālum inter Caesarem et Pompeium pugnatum est. Caesar vincit.
Event (English): The Battle of Pharsalus between Caesar and Pompey is fought. Caesar wins.

Event (Latin): Pharnaces, rex Ponti et filius Mithradatis VI a Iulio Caesare facile victus est
Event (English): Pharnaces, king of Pontus and son of Mithradates VI is easily defeated by Caesar

Event (Latin): Cleopatra VII regina Aegypta fit postquam Ptolemaios XIII a Iulio Caesare occisus est
Event (English): Cleopatra VII becomes queen of Egypt after Ptolemy XIII is killed by Caesar

Event (Latin): Factio Pompeii Magni, a Catone Minore ducta, in Africa victa est
Event (English): The Pompeiian Faction, led by Cato the Younger, is defeated in Africa

Event (Latin): Titus Labienus, olim legatus Caesaris in Galliā, mortuus est contrā Caesarem pugnāns apud Mundam in Hispania
Event (English): Caesar's former legate in Gaul, Titus Labienus, dies fighting against Caesar at the Battle of Munda in Hispania

Event (Latin): Kalendarium Iulianum adoptatum est
Event (English): The Julian Calendar is adopted

Event (Latin): Iulius Caesar dictatorem perpetuum declaratus est
Event (English): Julius Caesar is declared Dictator for life

Event (Latin): Idibus Martiis Gaius Iūlius Caesar ā senatoribus viginti et trēs vulneribus in curiā confossus est
Event (English): On the Ides of March, Julius Caesar is stabbed to death with 23 wounds by a group of senators

Event (Latin): Marcus Antonius, Marcus Aemilius Lepidus, et Gaius Octavius secundum triumviratum formavērunt
Event (English): Mark Antony, Marcus Aemilius Lepidus, and Octavian form the second triumvirate

Event (Latin): Aulus Hirtius octāvum et ultimum librum Bellī Gallicī Caesaris perfecit
Event (English): Aulus Hirtius finishes Book VII of Caesar's Gallic Wars

Event (Latin): Cicero in praescriptiōne secundī triumvirātī decapitatus est
Event (English): Cicero is beheaded in the prescriptions of the second triumvirate.

Event (Latin): Hortensia triumviratum secundum oratiōne praeclarā persuadet ut vectigal feminarum pecuniosarum obroget
Event (English): Hortensia in a famous speech persuades the Second Triumvirate to repeal a tax on wealthy women

Event (Latin): Brutus Cassiusque in proeliō apud Philippōs occiduntur
Event (English): Brutus and Cassius are killed at the battle of Philippi

Event (Latin): Livia, quae posthac uxor Augusti futūra esset, flilium Tiberium Claudium Neronem parit.
Event (English): Livia, who would later be the future wife of Augustus, gives birth to Tiberius.

Event (Latin): Proelium navale apud Actium inter Anontii Cleopatraeque copias et copias Octavianī pugnatum est.
Event (English): The naval Battle of Actium between the forces of Antony and Cleopatra and those of Octavian takes place.

Event (Latin): Marcus Antonius Cleopatraque sē interfecērunt.
Event (English): Marc Antony and Cleopatra commit suicide.

Event (Latin): Bellum Cantabrium in quō Rōmānī totam partem reliquam Hispaniam ā Gallīs capiunt incepit.
Event (English): The Cantabrian War, in which the Romans take control of the entire remaining part of Celtic Iberia begins.

Event (Latin): Templum Apollinis in monte Palatino aedificatum est.
Event (English): The Temple of Apollo on the Palatine hill is built.

Event (Latin): Augustus princeps fit et tribuniciam potestam perpetuam accipit.
Event (English): Augustus becomes "Princeps" (Emperor) taking the power of the Tribunes for life.

Event (Latin): Marcus Terentius Varro, qui magnum numerum librōrum de rebus variis vitae Romanae scripsit, mortuus est.
Event (English): Marcus Terentius Varro, who had written a large number of books about many topics of Roman life, dies.

Event (Latin): Augustus provinciam Galatiam imperiō Rōmānō addidit.
Event (English): Augustus annexes the province of Galatia.

Event (Latin): Nepos Augusti, nomine Marcellus, fīlius Octaviae quem Augustus volēbat post sē imperāre, mortuus est.
Event (English): Augustus's nephew, Marcellus, son of Octavia, whom Augustus wanted to rule after him, dies.

Event (Latin): Filia Augusti, nomine Iulia, a Marco Agrippa in matrimonium ducta est.
Event (English): Julia, the daughter of Augustus, marries Marcus Agrippa.

Event (Latin): Vergilius mortuus est. Post mortem Virgili, Augustus carmen epicum eius, Aeneidos, edidit.
Event (English): Vergil dies. After his death, Augustus publishes Virgil's epic poem, the Aeneid.

Event (Latin): Carmen nomine Ars Poetica ā Horatiō scriptum est.
Event (English): Horace writes the poem Ars Poetica.

Event (Latin): Marcus Agrippa morbo mortuus est
Event (English): Marcus Agrippa dies of fever

Event (Latin): Filia Augusti, nomine Iulia, a Tiberio in matrimonium ducta est.
Event (English): Julia, the daughter of Augustus, marries Tiberius.

Event (Latin): Theatrum Marcellī aedificatum est.
Event (English): The Theater of Marcellus is built.

Event (Latin): Pannonia in provinciam Illyricum inclusit.
Event (English): Pannonia is absorbed by the province of Illyricum.

Event (Latin): Q. Horatius Flaccus poeta, qui Carmen Saeculare scripserat, mortuus est.
Event (English): The poet Horace who wrote the Carmen Saeculare, dies.

Event (Latin): Ultimus annus descriptus librō Liviī, Ab Urbe Condita.
Event (English): The last year covered by Livy's Ab Urbe Condita.

Event (Latin): Tiberius ad īnsulam Rhodum abcessit, quia imperium noluit.
Event (English): Tiberius, not wanting imperium, retires to the island of Rhodes.

Event (Latin): Augustus "Pater Patriae" ā senātū nōminātus est.
Event (English): Augustus is declared "Pater Patriae" (Father of the Fatherland) by the senate.
